commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes personal publisher recovery handle comparisons and adds a mixed-case recovery regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main normalizes nextUserHandle to lower case, then compares it to the stored nextUser.handle with exact casing. I did not execute the regression locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A collaborator-authored, security-sensitive recovery fix should receive maintainer review rather than automated close or repair; I found no concrete code defect for a repair lane.

target.title: fix: normalize publisher recovery handle comparisons
target.body:
## Summary
- compare stored recovery user handles using canonical publisher-handle normalization
- retire a legacy mixed-case user handle before assigning the recovered publisher handle
- cover the production-shaped mixed-case recovery regression

## Proof
- before fix, the focused regression failed with `Next GitHub provider id does not belong to @gingiris-1031`
- after fix, `bunx vitest run convex/publishers.test.ts` passes all 76 tests

## Tests
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:types-build`
- `bun run ci:packages`
- `bun run ci:e2e-http`
target.author: patrick-erichsen
